summary: McKenzie reveals that for years he tracked whether users reached his purchase page from the trial version, but never tracked which specific trial restriction prompted the purchase. After realizing this oversight, he added tracking in just 8 minutes. The post illustrates how small, obvious instrumentation gaps can persist for years and cost valuable business intelligence.
key_points: Bingo Card Creator's free trial had three specific restrictions designed to drive purchases: no text import, no saving, and a 15-card print limit
McKenzie tracked trial-to-purchase conversions but never tracked which specific restriction triggered the purchase decision
The fix took only 8 minutes to implement, making the years-long oversight especially painful
Understanding which restrictions drive conversions is essential for optimizing trial-to-paid funnels
money_quotes: I have, for a long time, tracked whether people get to my purchasing page via coming from the trial or not — but I have never tracked from where in the trial.

It would be very, very helpful for me to know which of those restrictions are most effective at driving the sales… but I never thought of tracking it before, at least not enough to spend the 8 minutes it took me to add that in.
Do you know what drives people to upgrade from your trial?
key_insight: The most valuable analytics instrumentation is often trivially easy to implement but goes unbuilt for years because no one thinks to ask the question.
